title: nvidia-nemo/labs-molt
summary: A high-performance, PyTorch-native framework for agentic reinforcement learning, designed for scaling to frontier-class models using Ray, vLLM, and NVIDIA AutoModel.
risk_notes: Requires significant compute resources (NVIDIA GPUs) and complex dependencies like Ray and vLLM. Installation typically involves pip-based workflows; requires careful management of environment variables and potentially sensitive API keys for LLM-as-a-judge or external tool integration. The project is highly specialized for research and requires deep knowledge of distributed training architectures.

title: katanemo/plano
summary: Plano is an AI-native proxy and data plane built on Envoy that provides orchestration, observability, and guardrails for agentic applications.
risk_notes: Distributed as a Docker image or binary. It acts as a central proxy for LLM traffic, meaning it requires access to sensitive API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.) and handles all incoming/outgoing agent traffic. It is actively maintained by core Envoy contributors, but requires careful configuration of network policies and secret management.

title: getzep/graphiti
summary: Graphiti is a powerful open-source framework for building temporal knowledge graphs, allowing AI agents to maintain evolving, context-aware memory of facts and relationships over time.
risk_notes: Installed via standard Python pip packages. It requires integration with LLM providers (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic) and likely a vector database, necessitating API keys. It is actively maintained by a commercial entity (Zep) and is designed for integration into production agent pipelines, which requires careful management of data privacy and access control for the ingested context.

title: insforge/insforge
summary: InsForge is an all-in-one backend platform designed specifically for AI coding agents, providing integrated database, auth, storage, and compute capabilities via an MCP server or CLI.
risk_notes: The platform offers both a cloud-hosted version and a self-hosted option. As an agentic backend, it requires significant access to your infrastructure, including database credentials, storage buckets, and API keys for LLM providers. Self-hosting involves managing complex infrastructure (Postgres, Deno, etc.), which increases the attack surface if not properly secured.

title: shepherd-agents/shepherd
summary: A runtime substrate for AI agents that provides reversible execution traces, sandboxing, and copy-on-write workspaces to safely supervise and optimize agent behavior.
risk_notes: Installed via pip. Uses OS-level sandboxing (Seatbelt on macOS, Landlock on Linux) to restrict agent access. Requires API keys (e.g., Anthropic) for agent functionality, though it supports offline/keyless modes. The project is in early alpha, so APIs and security implementations are subject to change.

title: agrinman/tunnelto
summary: A high-performance, Rust-based CLI tool for exposing local web servers to the internet via secure tunnels, with support for self-hosting the server component.
risk_notes: Installs via cargo or brew. Requires network access to tunnel traffic from the public internet to your local machine. Self-hosting requires deploying a server component, which involves managing TCP/websocket ports. The project has not seen significant updates since 2022, suggesting it may be in maintenance mode.

title: atarity/deploy-your-own-saas
summary: A comprehensive, curated list of self-hostable alternatives to popular proprietary cloud services, categorized by function.
risk_notes: This is a directory, not a tool itself. The individual projects listed vary wildly in security posture and installation methods, ranging from simple Docker containers to complex Ansible playbooks. Users must evaluate each linked project's security, maintenance, and resource requirements individually before deployment.

title: getclawe/clawe
summary: A multi-agent coordination system that uses a Kanban-style interface to manage AI agents, allowing them to collaborate on tasks via a Convex backend and Docker-based infrastructure.
risk_notes: Requires Docker/Docker Compose for deployment and a Convex account for backend state management. It necessitates providing Anthropic/OpenAI API keys, which the agents will use to perform tasks. The system runs agents in isolated workspaces, but these agents have access to local files and the network to perform their duties. The project is very new (created Feb 2026), so it should be treated as experimental.

title: adminsyspro/proxcenter-ui
summary: ProxCenter is a comprehensive management platform for Proxmox VE and Proxmox Backup Server, offering multi-cluster orchestration, migration tools, and monitoring in a unified interface.
risk_notes: Installation uses a 'curl | sudo bash' script, which requires high-level system privileges. The tool requires administrative access to your Proxmox clusters to perform management tasks, meaning it holds significant control over your virtualized infrastructure. It is actively maintained with security scanning integrations (CodeQL, SonarCloud).

title: serial-studio/serial-studio
summary: A powerful, cross-platform telemetry dashboard for visualizing and interacting with hardware devices via serial, network, and industrial protocols.
risk_notes: Distributed as precompiled binaries for major OSs. Requires standard user permissions to access serial ports and network interfaces. The application supports scripting (JS/Lua) and external API integrations, which should be handled with caution if loading untrusted project files. Actively maintained with frequent updates.

title: winapps-org/winapps
summary: A powerful tool for running Windows applications seamlessly on Linux via RDP and virtualization (Docker/Libvirt/Podman), enabling native-like integration for apps like Microsoft Office and Adobe Creative Cloud.
risk_notes: Requires virtualization (KVM/Libvirt or Docker/Podman) and FreeRDP. It mounts the host's home directory into the Windows VM, which grants the Windows environment significant access to your Linux files. It is a complex setup involving system-level virtualization, so it requires careful configuration of the VM's security posture.

title: spacehuhntech/wifiduck
summary: A wireless keystroke injection platform that allows for remote execution of Ducky Script payloads via a web interface on ESP8266/Atmega32u4 hardware.
risk_notes: This is a hardware-based security tool designed for keystroke injection. It requires flashing firmware onto microcontrollers using the Arduino IDE. It operates as a HID device, meaning it can bypass standard OS security by mimicking a keyboard. It is intended for authorized security testing and educational purposes only.

title: safishamsi/graphify
summary: A powerful CLI tool that generates interactive knowledge graphs from local codebases and documentation, allowing for semantic querying of project structure without relying solely on vector embeddings.
risk_notes: Installed via 'uv' or 'pipx', which is a safe, isolated installation method. The tool requires read access to your local filesystem to parse code and documentation. It may require external API keys (e.g., OpenAI/Claude) if you choose to process non-code assets like images or PDFs via LLM, but core code parsing is local and deterministic.

title: pucherot/pi.alert
summary: A network monitoring and intrusion detection tool designed for home labs and Raspberry Pi environments that tracks connected devices and alerts on unauthorized access.
risk_notes: Installation uses a curl-to-bash script which requires root privileges to configure system services, web servers, and network scanning utilities. It requires access to network traffic and DHCP/DNS logs to function, which is standard for an IDS, but users should be aware of the broad system-level permissions granted during installation.

title: gobackup/gobackup
summary: A robust, dependency-free CLI tool for automating database and file backups to various cloud storage providers with built-in scheduling and web UI management.
risk_notes: Installation via 'curl | sh' is provided, which carries inherent security risks; manual binary installation or Homebrew is preferred. The tool requires access to database credentials and cloud storage API keys to function. It runs as a daemon and requires filesystem read access to the databases and files being backed up.

title: ramonvermeulen/whosthere
summary: A high-performance, unprivileged LAN discovery tool written in Go that provides both an interactive TUI and a daemon mode with an HTTP API.
risk_notes: Installs via standard package managers (brew, nix, yay) or Go. It runs in user-space without requiring root/sudo privileges. It performs network scanning (mDNS, SSDP, ARP) and optional port scanning, which is standard for network diagnostic tools. The project is actively maintained and written in memory-safe Go.

title: iamtomshaw/f1-race-replay
summary: An interactive Python-based visualization tool for Formula 1 race telemetry and event replays using the FastF1 library.
risk_notes: Standard Python project installation via pip. Requires network access to fetch race data from F1 APIs via the FastF1 library. No sensitive credentials required, but it creates a local cache directory (.fastf1-cache) in the project root. Maintenance is active, though it has a high number of open issues.

title: potatameister/paperknife
summary: A privacy-focused, zero-server PDF utility that performs all operations locally in the browser or via a Capacitor-based Android app using WebAssembly.
risk_notes: The tool is highly secure as it runs entirely client-side (browser or local app) with no server-side processing. It requires no network access to function, though it does require file system access to read/write the PDFs you choose to process. It is actively maintained and distributed via standard web hosting or APK/F-Droid.

title: tablecruncher/tablecruncher
summary: A high-performance C++ CSV editor capable of handling massive files with built-in JavaScript macro support.
risk_notes: Distributed as prebuilt binaries for major OSs or buildable via CMake/FLTK. As a local desktop application, it has standard user-level filesystem access. It includes a JavaScript engine (Duktape) for macros, which should be treated with caution if running untrusted CSV files or scripts. Maintenance is active following its recent transition to open source.

title: ilya-zlobintsev/lact
summary: LACT is a powerful Rust-based utility for monitoring and configuring AMD, Nvidia, and Intel GPUs on Linux, featuring a system daemon for headless control and a GTK4 GUI.
risk_notes: Requires root privileges via a systemd service (lactd) to interact with hardware interfaces. It manages GPU power and clock states, which carries inherent hardware risk if misconfigured. Installation is handled via standard package managers (pacman, apt, dnf) or Flatpak, which is safer than script-based installs. The daemon uses Unix socket permissions to restrict access to specific user groups.

title: jjang-ai/mlxstudio
summary: A native macOS desktop application and CLI tool for running local LLMs and image generation models using Apple's MLX framework, featuring high-performance inference and an OpenAI-compatible API server.
risk_notes: The project provides a signed/notarized DMG installer for macOS, which is a standard and safe installation method. The CLI version can be installed via pipx or uv, which isolates dependencies. As an AI inference tool, it requires local filesystem access to download and store model weights (which can be large) and network access to fetch models from HuggingFace. It runs a local API server on localhost:8000, which is standard for local LLM tools.

title: microck/ordinary-claude-skills
summary: A comprehensive, community-aggregated collection of Claude Skills and prompt-based tools organized by category for use with MCP servers or direct Claude integration.
risk_notes: The repository is a collection of text-based prompt files and scripts. It requires cloning the repository and potentially using the @modelcontextprotocol/server-filesystem to expose these files to an LLM. Users should review individual scripts before execution, as they may contain arbitrary code or instructions that could interact with local files or external APIs depending on the specific skill used.

title: khcrysalis/impactor
summary: A cross-platform, Rust-based iOS sideloading tool that simplifies signing and installing IPA files, managing certificates, and injecting tweaks.
risk_notes: Requires Apple ID credentials to function as a developer account for signing. Installs via Homebrew or Flatpak. Interacts with system-level USB drivers (usbmuxd on Linux, iTunes drivers on Windows) and requires local filesystem access to store signing keys and certificates. Active maintenance, but requires caution as it handles sensitive authentication tokens.

title: confident-ai/deepteam
summary: DeepTeam is a comprehensive, local-first framework for red teaming LLM applications, offering automated testing for vulnerabilities like prompt injection, PII leakage, and various security bypasses.
risk_notes: Installed via pip. Requires API keys for the LLMs used during evaluation (e.g., OpenAI, Anthropic). It runs locally, but by design, it performs adversarial probing against your own AI agents, which requires careful configuration to avoid unintended side effects on production systems. The project is actively maintained and built on the established DeepEval framework.

title: ai4finance-foundation/finrl-trading
summary: FinRL-X is a modular, AI-native quantitative trading framework designed for building, backtesting, and executing algorithmic trading strategies using reinforcement learning and traditional finance models.
risk_notes: Requires Python environment setup via pip. The application handles sensitive financial API keys (e.g., Alpaca) and performs live market execution, which carries significant financial risk. It requires network access to market data providers and brokerage APIs. The codebase is actively maintained and uses standard Pydantic configurations for security.

title: patoles/agent-flow
summary: A powerful visualization tool for Claude Code and Codex that provides real-time, interactive node-graph debugging for AI agent orchestration.
risk_notes: Installs via npm/pnpm. Requires read access to local agent session logs (typically in ~/.claude or ~/.codex) and sets up HTTP hooks to intercept agent events. It runs as a local web server or VS Code extension; no root access required, but it does monitor and process your AI agent's internal reasoning and tool-call data.

title: bastillion-io/bastillion
summary: A web-based SSH bastion host that provides centralized key management, multi-session terminal broadcasting, and 2FA for managing remote server fleets.
risk_notes: Runs as a Java application (requires JRE 21). It acts as a central point of failure and control for SSH access, requiring high-privilege access to target systems to manage authorized_keys. It is a commercial product with a free tier (up to 5 systems), which introduces a dependency on the vendor's licensing server/logic. Security posture is generally good (TLS, 2FA, CSRF protection), but it requires careful network isolation as it is a high-value target.

title: nvidia/audio2face-3d
summary: A central hub for NVIDIA's Audio2Face-3D technology, providing SDKs, training frameworks, and plugins for integrating high-fidelity AI-driven facial animation into 3D pipelines.
risk_notes: Requires significant hardware resources (NVIDIA GPU recommended). Installation involves Docker containers or C++ SDK integration, which requires standard development environment permissions. Models are pulled from Hugging Face. No dangerous 'curl | sudo' patterns, but requires careful management of large model weights and potential dependency conflicts in Python/C++ environments.

title: ierror/synaps-cad
summary: An AI-powered 3D CAD IDE written in Rust that integrates an OpenSCAD code editor with a Bevy-based 3D viewport and LLM-driven design iteration.
risk_notes: Distributed as pre-built binaries or via cargo build. Requires API keys for cloud AI providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.) or local Ollama access. The application requires network access for AI communication and filesystem access to read/write CAD files. macOS users must manually remove quarantine attributes due to lack of code signing.

title: eylandoo/openvpn_webpanel_manager
summary: A comprehensive, self-hosted web management panel for multiple VPN protocols including OpenVPN, WireGuard, Cisco AnyConnect, and L2TP/IPsec, featuring multi-node support and a RESTful API.
risk_notes: Installation requires running a script via 'curl | sudo bash' on Ubuntu 22.04, which grants the application full root access to the server. The panel interacts directly with system services (systemctl) and configuration files, meaning any vulnerability in the Flask application could lead to full system compromise. It is intended for dedicated server environments.

title: buildermethods/design-os
summary: A structured design and planning framework designed to bridge the gap between product vision and AI-assisted code generation by creating formal specs before implementation.
risk_notes: The project is a TypeScript-based tool that functions as a design process framework. It is actively maintained and does not appear to require invasive system permissions or root access. Usage involves following the documentation on their website, which likely requires standard npm/node workflows. Users should be aware that it is tied to a commercial ecosystem (Builder Methods), which may involve future paid tiers or proprietary integrations.

title: yuezk/globalprotect-openconnect
summary: A robust, cross-platform GlobalProtect VPN client for Linux that supports modern authentication methods like SSO, MFA, and YubiKey via OpenConnect.
risk_notes: Requires root/sudo privileges to manage network interfaces and routing tables. The project is actively maintained and provides native packages (DEB, RPM, AUR), which is safer than curl-to-bash scripts. Note that while the core service is open source, the GUI wrapper is proprietary, which may be a consideration for strict open-source advocates.

title: ui-layouts/ui-tools
summary: A collection of UI component generators and design utilities including mesh gradients, shadow generators, and SVG clip-path tools for web developers.
risk_notes: This is a library of frontend components and utility hooks. Installation is via standard npm packages (motion, clsx, tailwind-merge). It runs entirely in the browser/client-side and requires no special system permissions, root access, or backend secrets.

title: tiger-ai-lab/openresearcher
summary: OpenResearcher is an open-source agentic model and framework specifically designed for long-horizon deep research tasks, providing a complete pipeline including datasets, training recipes, and evaluation tools.
risk_notes: Requires significant local compute resources (GPU) for model inference and training. Installation typically involves standard Python/pip workflows. As an agentic tool, it is designed to perform web browsing and data retrieval, which requires careful management of network access and potential exposure to untrusted web content.

title: russellenvy/three-man-team
summary: A structured multi-agent workflow for Claude Code that organizes AI coding tasks into Architect, Builder, and Reviewer roles to improve output quality and reduce token waste.
risk_notes: Installs via git clone and a local setup script. It requires access to your project files and interacts with Claude Code, meaning it will consume your API tokens. It operates within your local filesystem and executes commands as the user running the CLI, so it has the same permissions as your terminal session.

title: casberryindia/physics-notebook
summary: An interactive educational platform for physics simulations using vanilla JavaScript, HTML5 Canvas, and MathJax.
risk_notes: The project is a static web application with no complex installation requirements. It runs locally via a simple HTTP server. It does not require root access or sensitive system permissions, though it includes links to external AI services which may require user-provided API keys or accounts.

title: sozercan/kaset
summary: A native macOS client for YouTube and YouTube Music featuring deep system integration, Apple Intelligence support, and extensibility via WebKit extensions.
risk_notes: Installed via Homebrew or manual download. As an unofficial client, it requires logging into a Google account, granting it access to your YouTube history, subscriptions, and personal data. The app is currently unsigned, requiring manual security overrides on macOS. It supports WebKit extensions, which could theoretically access web content.

title: seemoo-lab/openhaystack
summary: A framework that enables tracking of custom Bluetooth devices using Apple's Find My network, utilizing reverse-engineered protocols and a macOS application.
risk_notes: Requires significant system-level modifications, including disabling Gatekeeper and installing a custom Apple Mail plugin to access private APIs. The project is experimental and involves reverse-engineered protocols, meaning it may break with macOS updates. It requires access to the macOS keychain to store private keys.

title: insidegui/virtualbuddy
summary: A powerful GUI for managing macOS and Linux virtual machines on Apple Silicon, leveraging Apple's Virtualization framework for high-performance testing environments.
risk_notes: Distributed as a signed macOS application (DMG/App). Requires full disk access and virtualization entitlements to manage VMs. It is actively maintained by a well-known developer in the Apple ecosystem. No dangerous install scripts; standard macOS app installation.

title: tickernelz/opencode-mem
summary: A persistent memory plugin for AI coding agents that uses a local SQLite and vector database to provide long-term context retention across sessions.
risk_notes: Installed as an OpenCode plugin via configuration file; requires local filesystem access for data storage (~/.opencode-mem/data) and network access to download embedding models and communicate with AI providers (OpenAI/Anthropic). Requires API keys for LLM features. The project is actively maintained and uses standard npm/bun workflows.

title: dchristl/macless-haystack
summary: A self-hosted solution that enables the creation of custom FindMy-compatible tracking devices using ESP32 or NRF5x hardware without requiring an Apple Mac.
risk_notes: Requires Docker for deployment. The service necessitates providing Apple ID credentials and 2FA tokens to an 'Anisette' server to interface with Apple's FindMy network. This involves significant trust in the third-party Anisette implementation and the project's handling of authentication data. The project is actively maintained but involves reverse-engineered protocols.

title: anomalyco/openauth
summary: A modern, standards-based, self-hostable OAuth 2.0 identity provider designed for easy deployment on serverless platforms like Cloudflare Workers or AWS Lambda.
risk_notes: Installed via npm/bun as a library dependency. It requires sensitive configuration (client secrets for providers like GitHub/Google) and access to a KV store (DynamoDB/Cloudflare KV) for token management. As an auth provider, it is a high-value target; security depends on proper implementation of the storage layer and environment variable management. Actively maintained by the SST team.

title: sengchor/kokraf
summary: A browser-based 3D modeling application built with Three.js and Supabase for collaborative editing.
risk_notes: The project is a client-side web application that can be run locally via a simple static server or accessed via the provided URL. It relies on Supabase for backend services, which may require API keys if self-hosted. It does not require root privileges or system-level installation, but it is outside the scope of the user's interest in developer tools, automation, or home-lab infrastructure.

title: opendronemap/odm
summary: A powerful, open-source command-line toolkit for photogrammetry that generates 3D models, point clouds, and orthophotos from aerial imagery.
risk_notes: Distributed primarily via Docker, which requires root-level access to the Docker daemon. The tool processes local filesystem data mounted into the container. It is highly active and well-maintained, but users should be aware that it performs intensive computational tasks that can stress hardware.

title: firaslatrech/reqlog
summary: A zero-config, framework-agnostic development dashboard for real-time HTTP request inspection and replay, supporting Node.js (Express, NestJS, Fastify) and Go.
risk_notes: Installed via standard package managers (npm/go get). It runs as middleware within your application process, meaning it has access to all request/response data, including headers and bodies. It is explicitly designed for development and includes a safety mechanism to block execution in production environments by default.

title: felixrieseberg/windows95
summary: An Electron-based emulator that runs Windows 95 as a desktop application.
risk_notes: Distributed as pre-compiled binaries (exe, deb, rpm, zip). As an Electron app, it runs with the permissions of the user account. It is a novelty project rather than a developer tool or utility, and it lacks relevance to the user's focus on automation, data pipelines, or modern home-lab infrastructure.

title: kylan02/face_looker
summary: A tool that uses AI (via Replicate) to generate a grid of face images with varying gaze directions, which can then be used to create interactive cursor-tracking UI components.
risk_notes: Requires a Replicate API token to function. The Python script interacts with the local filesystem to read input images and write output grids. It uses standard pip installation for dependencies. The project is very new and relies on third-party AI services, so long-term maintenance may be tied to the availability of the specific Replicate model.

title: psimaker/vaultsync
summary: A privacy-focused iOS app that enables peer-to-peer synchronization of Obsidian vaults using Syncthing, with an optional sidecar for background wake-ups.
risk_notes: The iOS app is installed via the App Store. The optional 'Cloud Relay' sidecar uses a curl-to-shell script for installation, which requires access to your Syncthing configuration files. The app itself requires access to the Obsidian file sandbox on iOS. The project is actively maintained and uses an open-source license.

title: openhd/openhd
summary: OpenHD is a high-performance, low-latency digital video and telemetry transmission system for UAVs and robotics, utilizing standard Wi-Fi hardware in a custom broadcast mode.
risk_notes: Installation typically involves flashing a custom OS image provided by the project's Image Builder, which replaces the standard Raspberry Pi OS. It requires low-level hardware access to Wi-Fi adapters and operates as a dedicated system, meaning it is not intended to run alongside other general-purpose applications on the same hardware. It is actively maintained with a strong community presence.

title: rampstackco/claude-skills
summary: A comprehensive library of 103 modular 'Claude Skills' designed to standardize agentic workflows across the entire web development and product lifecycle.
risk_notes: Installed via the Claude Code CLI using '/plugin install'. It operates within the Claude agent environment, meaning it inherits the permissions granted to the Claude agent, including potential access to your local filesystem and any MCP servers (like Ahrefs) you explicitly connect. The project is actively maintained and follows Anthropic's official agent skills framework.

title: xpavle00/habo
summary: A privacy-focused, cross-platform habit tracker built with Flutter that supports end-to-end encrypted sync and self-hosting via Supabase.
risk_notes: The project is actively maintained. Self-hosting requires setting up a Supabase instance and managing API keys within the app. The app requires standard mobile permissions (biometrics, storage) and network access for sync. The code is open-source (GPL-3.0), allowing for audit of the E2EE implementation.

title: andrewjiang/palantir-for-family-trips
summary: A React-based dashboard for visualizing family trip logistics with a 'command center' aesthetic.
risk_notes: Standard npm install process. Requires a Google Maps API key which will incur billing on your Google Cloud account. The project is a single-purpose UI experiment with no backend, meaning it is not a general-purpose tool or automation platform.

title: honeyhoneyteam/esp32-marauder-double-barrel-5g
summary: A specialized hardware expansion board for the Flipper Zero that adds dual-band 5GHz/2.4GHz Wi-Fi, GPS, and Sub-GHz capabilities via integrated ESP32 and BW16 chipsets.
risk_notes: This is a physical hardware device. Usage requires flashing firmware binaries to the device and installing Flipper App Packages (FAPs) onto the Flipper Zero's SD card. It interacts with wireless protocols (Wi-Fi, Sub-GHz) and requires manual GPIO configuration on the Flipper. No software installation on a host PC is required, but it involves physical hardware modification/expansion.

title: jerbouma/financedatabase
summary: A comprehensive, community-driven database of over 300,000 financial symbols including equities, ETFs, and cryptocurrencies, designed for data analysis and integration with financial toolkits.
risk_notes: Installed via standard pip package. It is a static data library and does not require elevated privileges, network access, or API keys to function, making it very safe for local development or data pipelines.

title: stengo/deskpad
summary: DeskPad is a macOS utility that creates a virtual display, allowing users to share a specific, manageable workspace instead of their entire physical screen.
risk_notes: Distributed as a macOS application via Homebrew cask or direct binary download. Requires 'Screen Recording' permissions to function, which is standard for virtual display tools. The project is actively maintained and open source, but as a GUI app, it runs with standard user-level permissions.

title: ashishps1/awesome-engineering-articles
summary: A well-organized, curated list of high-quality engineering blog posts from major tech companies, categorized by company and topic.
risk_notes: This is a documentation-only repository containing markdown files. There is no code to execute, no installation required, and no security risk associated with accessing the content.

title: dnscrypt/encrypted-dns-server
summary: A high-performance, zero-maintenance proxy for running an encrypted DNS server, supporting DNSCrypt, Anonymized DNSCrypt, and DoH.
risk_notes: Requires root/sudo privileges to bind to standard ports (e.g., 443). Installation via precompiled binary or cargo is standard. The application manages sensitive cryptographic keys in a state file which must be secured. Maintained by a highly reputable developer in the DNS security space.

title: mhss1/mybrain
summary: An all-in-one, privacy-focused Android productivity app that integrates AI assistants with local data management like tasks, notes, and calendars.
risk_notes: Distributed via F-Droid or GitHub APK releases. It is a mobile app, so it requires standard Android permissions (storage, calendar, etc.). It is privacy-focused and local-first, but using the AI features requires providing your own API keys for services like Gemini or GPT. The project is actively maintained.

title: fer0n/unwatched
summary: A privacy-focused, RSS-based YouTube client for Apple platforms that eliminates algorithmic feeds and autoplay in favor of a manual queue.
risk_notes: Distributed via the Apple App Store, which provides a sandboxed environment. Building from source requires a personal YouTube Data API key, which is standard for third-party YouTube clients. The project is actively maintained and uses standard Swift/Apple development practices.

title: jayantdevkar/claude-code-karma
summary: A local-first dashboard for visualizing and analyzing Claude Code session data, providing insights into token usage, tool calls, and agent activity.
risk_notes: The tool runs locally and reads from the ~/.claude/ directory. It requires read access to your local session logs. It does not appear to require external API keys or network transmission of your data, as it is a local-first dashboard. Installation typically involves standard Python/Node package management.
